name: Agent Handoff Protocol
title: Universal Agent Handoff Protocol (UCO)
description: JSON-based protocol for exchanging session context and task state between AI agents across generic IDEs.
anti_patterns: Overloading the `state_tree` with large binary data; use file links instead.
Bypassing UCO for out-of-band communication between agents, leading to context loss.
Failing to validate UCO schema on receipt, which can cause downstream agent crashes.

axiomAlignment.A1_verifiability: UCO structure allows for integrity checks of the transferred state.
axiomAlignment.A3_transparency: The handoff object explicitly lists active workflows and constraints.
axiomAlignment.A5_consistency: Standardized schema ensures predictable handoffs between different agent personas.

task_id.description: Global identifier for the task (e.g., Plane ID).

session_id.description: Unique identifier for the current interaction session.

sender_did.description: DID of the agent initiating the handoff.

state_tree.description: The current state of the task, including variables, file buffers, and tool outputs.

constraints.description: Operating limits or rules to be inherited by the receiver.

artifacts.description: Relative paths to artifacts associated with the session.

steps: Capture: Sender serializes current state into UCO.
Transmit: Sender posts UCO to shared memory or message bus.
Resume: Receiver hydrates local state from UCO and continues workflow.

best_practices: Always include the Plane task ID in `task_id` for cross-system tracking.
Sanitize `state_tree` to remove temporal internal objects that cannot be serialized.
Log every handoff event in the global agent audit log.
